QF.id: VIII Introduction to Clearing and Settlement System_75

question.text: Effective February 1, 2025, SEBI removed the calendar spread margin benefit on:

answer.text: Contracts expiring on the same day, to address basis risks and speculative volatility on expiry days
All calendar spread positions regardless of expiry date
Calendar spreads where the near-month contract has more than 7 days to expiry

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI removed the calendar spread margin offset for contracts expiring on the SAME day from Feb 1, 2025. Earlier, positions in the same underlying but different strikes/expiries on the same expiry day received margin offsets. This was removed to address speculative activity concentrated on expiry days. **The Trap:** Calendar spread benefits still exist for spreads across DIFFERENT expiry months. Only SAME-day expiry spreads lost the benefit. **Cross-Exam Context:** Recent SEBI measures to curb speculative activity are topical exam questions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Same-day expiry spreads = no margin benefit since Feb 2025. Cross-month spreads still get margin offsets.
